openmeter/namespace/namespacedriver.purpose: Provides the NamespaceDecoder abstraction for extracting namespace context from HTTP requests. The sole implementation, StaticNamespaceDecoder, always returns a fixed string — used in self-hosted single-tenant deployments where every request belongs to the default namespace.

patterns.name: NamespaceDecoder interface contract
patterns.description: All namespace-resolution strategies implement GetNamespace(ctx) (string, bool). Returning false signals 'namespace not found' and callers must reject; returning true with an empty string is a bug (callers treat empty as valid).
patterns.example: type StaticNamespaceDecoder string; func (d StaticNamespaceDecoder) GetNamespace(ctx context.Context) (string, bool) { return string(d), true }

patterns.name: Named string type for zero-boilerplate decoders
patterns.description: StaticNamespaceDecoder is a plain named string type so config values cast directly without a constructor. Stateful decoders should be structs; pure-string resolvers follow the named-type pattern.

patterns.name: Zero-import leaf package
patterns.description: decoder.go imports only 'context'. This package sits at the infrastructure/transport boundary and must stay free of openmeter domain imports to avoid cycles with callers like openmeter/server/router.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the NamespaceDecoder interface and StaticNamespaceDecoder. The entire package is one file.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add business logic, state, or domain imports. Do not return true with an empty string from any GetNamespace implementation.

anti_patterns: Importing openmeter domain packages (billing, customer, entitlement, meter) — creates import cycles with callers.
Adding initialization logic or fields to StaticNamespaceDecoder — it is intentionally a plain named string.
Returning (true, "") from GetNamespace — callers treat an empty namespace as valid and silently misscope queries.
Placing multi-tenant namespace resolution logic here — dynamic lookup belongs in a separate decoder implementation.
Adding HTTP middleware or Chi handler code — request plumbing belongs in openmeter/server.

decisions.decision: StaticNamespaceDecoder is a named string type rather than a struct.
decisions.rationale: Avoids constructor boilerplate for the common self-hosted case where the namespace is a plain config string.

decisions.decision: NamespaceDecoder returns (string, bool) rather than (string, error).
decisions.rationale: Namespace absence is a routing concern, not an error; bool cleanly signals 'not found' without forcing callers to inspect error types.

code_examples.scenario: Implement a static namespace decoder from a config value
code_examples.code:
import "github.com/openmeterio/openmeter/openmeter/namespace/namespacedriver"

decoder := namespacedriver.StaticNamespaceDecoder(conf.Namespace.Default)

ns, ok := decoder.GetNamespace(ctx)
if !ok {
  http.Error(w, "namespace not found", http.StatusUnauthorized)
  return
}
